## Detected Signals

These are factual observations about what was found in the project. "Not detected" means

file and none was located.

## Standards Checklist

Review each item against `standards/api-design.md`. Items marked **(Manual)** require
reading the spec or source code — they cannot be auto-detected.

### API Specification (§1)

- [ ] OpenAPI spec present — `{{CHECK_SPEC_FILE}}`
- [ ] Spec is OpenAPI 3.x (not Swagger 2.x) — `{{CHECK_SPEC_VERSION}}`
- [ ] Spec has title and version in info block — `{{CHECK_SPEC_INFO}}`

### Versioning (§2)

- [ ] API versioning strategy evident — `{{CHECK_VERSIONING}}`
- [ ] (Manual) Version is in the URL path (`/v1/`), not a header

### Resource Naming (§3)

- [ ] (Manual) Resources use plural nouns (`/users`, not `/user` or `/getUsers`)
- [ ] (Manual) Paths are lowercase and kebab-case

### HTTP Methods and Status Codes (§4)

- [ ] (Manual) Correct HTTP verbs used (GET read, POST create, PUT/PATCH update, DELETE remove)
- [ ] (Manual) Status codes are appropriate (201 for creation, 400 for bad input, etc.)
- [ ] (Manual) No `200 OK` returned with an error body

### Request and Response Shapes (§5)

- [ ] (Manual) No raw database objects returned directly from endpoints
- [ ] (Manual) Response shapes are consistent across endpoints

### Error Responses (§6)

- [ ] (Manual) Error responses follow a consistent format (code, message, details)
- [ ] (Manual) Error bodies contain actionable information, not stack traces

### Documentation (§7)

- [ ] Descriptions present in spec — `{{CHECK_SPEC_DESCRIPTIONS}}`
- [ ] Examples present in spec — `{{CHECK_SPEC_EXAMPLES}}`
- [ ] API documentation file present — `{{CHECK_API_DOCS}}`
- [ ] (Manual) Every endpoint has a description, not just a summary

### Reusable Schemas (§8)

- [ ] Components/schemas defined — `{{CHECK_SPEC_COMPONENTS}}`
- [ ] (Manual) Common types (errors, pagination) use `$ref` to shared schemas

### Authentication (§9)

- [ ] (Manual) Auth scheme documented in spec (`securitySchemes`)
- [ ] (Manual) Endpoints declare their security requirements

### Hygiene

- [ ] API client collection present — `{{CHECK_CLIENT_COLLECTION}}`
- [ ] .gitignore present — `{{CHECK_GITIGNORE}}`
